\begin{table}[!t]
  \centering
  \caption{Accreditation treatment of subject-component balance in the 36 completed dual-subject
  speciality~014 programmes (36 cases; 30 of 36 carried at least one
  substantive remark on the distribution between the primary and additional subject, 118 remarks in
  total, raised overwhelmingly by the expert group (60) and the Sectoral Expert Council (56)).
  Rows distinguish cases in which the expert group or Sectoral Expert Council exerted explicit
  parity/balance pressure (treating an uneven primary/additional credit ratio as a deficiency, or
  requiring \textit{parytetnist'}) from those in which they did not. Outcome is the final National-Agency
  decision. Retrieved May~2026 from the NAQA portal \citep{NAQA:Portal2026}.}
  \label{tab:accred-remarks}
  \footnotesize
  \begin{tabular}{lrrrrr}
    \toprule
    & Accredited & Conditional & Refused & Total & Non-clean \\
    \midrule
    Parity/balance pressure & 13 & 9 & 1 & 23 & 43\,\% \\
    No such pressure & 11 & 2 & 0 & 13 & 15\,\% \\
    \midrule
    \textit{All} & 24 & 11 & 1 & 36 & 33\,\% \\
    \bottomrule
  \end{tabular}

  \vspace{2pt}
  {\footnotesize Fisher's exact test, parity-pressure $\times$ non-clean outcome: $p=0.143$.}
\end{table}
